clue2Characteristics: Deliberate, obvious exaggeration
Used for emphasis or dramatic effect, not to deceive
Both speaker and listener understand it is not literal
Common in everyday speech, jokes, and persuasive writing
clue3Note: Unlike a word that means a lie meant to deceive, the hidden word describes exaggeration that everyone involved recognises as exaggeration — it is a tool for emphasis, not an attempt to mislead.
clue4ContextSentence: When the coach said he had told the team a million times to pass the ball, nobody thought he meant it literally — it was obvious ________.

relatedGuesses.distinction: "Exaggeration" is the everyday, general word; the hidden word is the more formal, literary term specifically used to describe this technique.
"Lie" implies an intent to deceive; the hidden word is understood by everyone as not literal, so it does not deceive anyone.
"Metaphor" compares two unlike things without claiming literal truth in a different way; the hidden word specifically exaggerates scale or degree for emphasis.
"Joke" is a broad word for anything said for humour; the hidden word is specifically the technique of exaggerating for emphasis, which may or may not be funny.

intendedMeaning: to extend known data or a trend beyond the observed range in order to estimate what is likely to hold in new or future cases
definition: To extend known data or a trend beyond the observed range in order to estimate what is likely to hold true in new or future cases.
clue1Distinction: The hidden word is not about guessing randomly — it describes taking data you already have and carefully EXTENDING its pattern to estimate something beyond what was directly measured.
clue2Characteristics: Extends a known pattern or trend beyond the range that was actually measured
Used to estimate future values or unmeasured cases
Based on existing data, not pure speculation
Common in statistics, science, and forecasting
clue3Note: Unlike a word that means to guess without evidence, the hidden word means using EXISTING data and extending its pattern logically into territory that has not yet been directly observed.
clue4ContextSentence: Using five years of measured growth, the ecologist ________d that the coral reef would recover fully within another decade if conditions stayed the same.

relatedGuesses.distinction: "Guess" implies little or no evidence behind the estimate; the hidden word specifically means extending an existing data trend, not guessing blindly.
"Predict" is a general word for forecasting the future; the hidden word specifically describes the METHOD — extending a known pattern beyond its measured range.
"Calculate" implies working out a precise value from given numbers; the hidden word specifically involves extending a pattern into unmeasured territory, which carries more uncertainty.
"Assume" often means taking something for granted without much justification; the hidden word is a more careful, evidence-based extension of an existing trend.

modelSentence: From just three years of sales figures, the analyst extrapolated a five-year forecast that convinced investors to fund the expansion.
failExplanation: The hidden word was "extrapolate" — to extend known data or a trend beyond the observed range to estimate future or new cases. Your guesses probably described plain guessing or predicting; "extrapolate" specifically means extending an existing pattern based on real data.
